_comment_navigation_duplication_policy: Some documents intentionally appear in multiple navigation groups. These are alternate navigation entry points — not duplicate documentation. Examples: SOLUTION_STATUS, 50-audit-reports/BUSINESS_OS_EXECUTION_ROADMAP, individual Sprint PRDs. The repository remains the single source of truth. Navigation duplication is permitted only when it improves usability. Document duplication is never permitted.
_comment_future_module_navigation_standa: Repository Navigation Standard v2.0. Each module group MOD-XXX <Name> exposes items in this order (only those existing on disk): Overview, Baseline, Publication, WEB-XXX — Web Solution Design, MOB-XXX — Mobile Solution Design, API-XXX — API Solution Design, CPC-XXX — Cross-Platform Certification, VR-XXX — Verification, Sprint Plan, SPR-MOD-XXX-NNN sprint PRDs. Item labels inside a module group do NOT repeat the module name (the group header carries it). IRR (Implementation Readiness Review) is a phase gate, not a module artifact, and MUST NOT appear inside any module group; IRR and Delivery artifacts (Implementation Planning, Engineering Execution/Completion, System Verification, User Acceptance, Release Readiness, Production Release, Post-Release Verification) remain centralized under global Delivery groups. Modules SHALL be ordered by Module ID (MOD-001 → MOD-019, then MOD-020+). No placeholders. No dead links.
